name: Tech Unity Cookout
description:
How pleasant it is when the people of tech come together in unity!

Free admission. Food will be provided!

After a year of talking about it, we’re finally doing it: a chill summer cookout for everyone in Baltimore tech. No booths, no buzzwords — just food, sun, and a mic for any group that wants to say hi.

Participating groups

* **Code Collective**
* **Baltimore Node Makerspace**
* **Baltimore Tech**
* **Brady UX**
* **DevOps Columbia**
* **Blk Tech Connect**
* **Baltimore Indie Game Devs**

In the case of inclement weather, the event will occur either virtually or at another venue.

location.name: TBD - location has not been decided yet

name: Baltimore Code & Coffee Demo Day
description:
Our Demo Day is a chance for members of the local tech community to show off the cool projects they’ve been working on. Come and enjoy our four participant’s ten minute demos with five minutes for questions.

After the demos, each of the participants will have their own table for you to ask more questions about their work in depth. After 30 mins of this, we will have a vote for the winner.

**SCHEDULE:**
Networking - 11:00am-11:30am
Demos - 11:30am-12:30pm
Table Talk - 12:30pm-1:00pm
Vote 1:00pm-1:10pm
Networking - 1:10pm - 2:00pm

**Venue:**
Reveille Grounds

**PARKING:**
Street parking is plentiful and free on Severn, Bush, and Bayard Streets, especially after 3:30pm and on weekends:The code for the walk-in gate to the Courtyard is 0250#

Once through the gate, Building C is the first building on your left, and Reveille is on the 2nd Floor.

See the venue website (https://www.reveillegrounds.com/location) for more information

location.name: Reveille Grounds
location.address: 1401 Severn St Building C, Suite C250

name: [IN-PERSON/ONLINE] Game Dev Meetup (East Columbia Branch)
description:
**IN-PERSON + ONLINE**

*In-person is at the **East Columbia Branch Library** in the Marvin Thomas Room.*

Join us **IN-PERSON** or ONLINE. Anyone with an interest in game design and development is welcome, from beginners to veteran professionals.

We will be meeting in the Marvin Thomas Room at the East Columbia Branch Library (6600 Cradlerock Way). (Not an official Howard County Library System event.)

If you want to attend virtually, the Discord server link is provided in the main monthly (1st of the month) meetup details. An organizer will ensure that a laptop is provided on-site to connect the Discord attendees with those physically on-site. It takes us a few minutes to set up the projector, so hold tight when you join the Discord voice chat channel. We'll be there soon!

name: [IN-PERSON] Summer Game Dev Picnic
description:
Come join us for the Baltimore Indie Game Devs potluck picnic at BIG member Allen Lambert's house in Marriottsville just west of Baltimore. This picnic is for all local game devs in the Baltimore-Washington area. Allen will be manning the grill, and there will be outdoor games as well as tabletop games to play.

If you'd like to bring something to the potluck, please add it in the Comments section.

Notes:

* This is a NO ALCOHOL event.
* In the event of rain, we may postpone this event.
* **We'll send the street address to RSVP'd attendees one week before the picnic and the day before the picnic. Please send a message to Allen Lambert or Tony Li if you'd like the address sooner.**

name: Bitcoin Pizza Day Party with PizzaDAO
description:
Join us for a delicious celebration of Bitcoin Pizza Day, brought to you by PizzaDAO! 🍕💸

On this historic day, we honor the legendary first real-world Bitcoin transaction: when Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for two pizzas in 2010. That single cheesy exchange ignited a worldwide movement and earned a permanent slice in crypto history!

Join us for a delicious celebration of Bitcoin Pizza Day, brought to you by PizzaDAO! 🍕💸
On this historic day, we honor the legendary first real-world Bitcoin transaction: when Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for two pizzas in 2010. That single cheesy exchange ignited a worldwide movement and earned a permanent slice in crypto history!
🔥 **What to Expect:**
✅ **Casual networking** – Meet fellow crypto enthusiasts, builders, and investors.
✅ DeFi Demo – Presentation by local web3 developer who has built up $5 million in TVL
✅ **Good vibes, great beer** – No pitches, no panels, just **fun and great conversation** over drinks.
🚗 **Heads up: Parking is street parking only, so plan accordingly!**
